_comment: Curated sealed-product membership data, merged into `sealed_contents` ONLY for products
MTGJSON leaves with no contents. MTGJSON (AllPrintings.json) is the authoritative source;
this file fills genuine upstream gaps — products whose `contents` are un-authored so their
cards would otherwise show no sealed products at all. See api/src/mtgjson/fallback.rs and the
'Sealed-product contents' trade-off in CLAUDE.md.
A fallback product is applied per-product only when MTGJSON emitted ZERO rows for it, so a
product MTGJSON later starts describing silently supersedes this file (no code change).
Exception: an entry with `supplement: true` merges its `contents` ALONGSIDE upstream's rows
even when MTGJSON describes the product — for an axis upstream is missing (e.g. a deck
reference that resolves to no deck data, or land packs upstream keeps textual). By default,
upstream rows are untouched, duplicates dedup away (self-retiring per card), and the entry's
`components` still only fill a product upstream gives no composition. A supplement may also
set `override_memberships: true` when upstream finds the right cards but assigns the wrong
certainty; contradictory memberships are then removed only for the curated cards.
Cards are keyed by (set, number) matching cards.set_code/collector_number; `name` is
documentation only. `membership` is contains | booster | variable (the 'found in / can be
pulled from / may be in' buckets). `foil` marks a foil-only inclusion.
`components` (optional, alongside `contents`) is the structural composition — the
'what's in the box' line items. Each has a `kind` (sealed | deck | card | other), a display
`name`, and a `quantity`. A `sealed` component links a sub-product by its
`child_tcgplayer_product_id`; a `card` component links a card by `(child_set, child_number)`;
`deck` / `other` are textual. Applied only when MTGJSON gives the product no composition.
Editing this file changes its content hash, which the ingest folds into its version gate so
the merge re-runs on the next sync even if MTGJSON itself is unchanged.

products.name: Avatar: The Last Airbender - Commander's Bundle
note: SUPPLEMENT (issue #352): mtg-sealed-content now authors this bundle's contents as a `deck`
reference ('Commander's Bundle', set tla), plus the boosters and textual land packs.
That deck was initially absent, so the guaranteed staples + 2-of-10 pool vanished while
booster rows made the product look covered. MTGJSON now resolves the deck, but represents
all 13 staples as guaranteed `contains` cards because its deck shape cannot express that
10 form a random pool. `supplement` merges the curated cards despite upstream coverage;
`override_memberships` removes contradictory buckets only for those named cards, so the
pool remains `variable`. Upstream's composition and unrelated membership rows stay as-is.
The bundle guarantees three staples (Arcane Signet, Sol Ring, Swiftfoot Boots) and includes
two more drawn from a pool of ten — so the guaranteed three are `contains` and the pool is
`variable`, all the nonfoil borderless printing (Avatar 'Eternal' set tle, 305-317).
The two 15-card land packs (one traditional foil, one nonfoil) each hold 5 full-art Appa
basics (tla 287-291, one per color) + 10 default-frame basics (tla 282-286, two per color) —
per Wizards' 'Collecting Avatar' article, which explicitly notes the packs do NOT contain
the Avatar's Journey full-art cycle (tla 292-296) despite the packaging. Both finishes of
each land are `contains` rows (the foil pack carries the foil finish of the same printings).
Source: Wizards 'Collecting Avatar' article + retail listings.
`components` is the safety net for an upstream regression back to contents:null (today
upstream's own composition wins the usual gate): 9 Play Boosters (tcg 648640) + 1
Collector Booster (tcg 648646) + the three guaranteed borderless staples (Arcane Signet,
Sol Ring, Swiftfoot Boots; linked to tle 315-317) + 2 random borderless staples drawn from
the pool of ten (a textual `card` line, unlinkable since the two are randomised) + the land
packs, life counter and storage box. The guaranteed three and the pool mirror the `contents`
above. Source: Wizards 'Collecting Avatar' article + the Amazon/retail product listing.
